analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet invites the public to participate in an EU consultation on the Digital Services Act, focusing on freedom of expression and digital platforms, with an emphasis on shaping EU policy. Evaluation: EU1 (EU is a successful story) – Not promoted; no reference to history, overcoming obstacles, or past achievements. EU2 (EU is united) – Not promoted; no mention of member state unity or joint positions. EU3 (EU is useful for its citizens) – Promoted implicitly: it highlights a concrete EU policy process (DSA) addressing citizens’ concerns about online freedom and digital platforms, inviting public input, which portrays the EU as practical and responsive. EU4 (EU as avant-garde) – Promoted: positioning the EU as shaping cutting-edge digital regulation (DSA) suggests pioneering leadership in digital policy. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – Not promoted; no explicit framing in terms of democracy/human rights leadership globally. EU6 (EU contributes to a better world) – Not promoted; no global or external dimension. Conclusion: EU3 and EU4 apply.
